response: # PrimaryButton Accessibility Testing Plan > **For Claude:** Use a11y-planner protocol. Review with a11y-critic after implementation. > **Compliance target:** WCAG 2.2 AA > **Users who need accessibility:** Screen reader users (NVDA, JAWS, VoiceOver), keyboard-only users, low-vision users > **Assistive technologies:** NVDA (Windows), JAWS (Windows), VoiceOver (macOS), keyboard-only **Feature:** Accessibility test suite for an existing reusable `` React component (text label, optional leading icon, `disabled` state, `isLoading` state with `aria-busy`). **Risk Level:** Low **Component Type:** Button (native ` ``` --- ## Interaction Pattern Design ### Interactive Elements Table | Widget | APG Pattern | Keyboard | ARIA | WCAG | |--------|-------------|----------|------|------| | PrimaryButton (default) | [Button](https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/button/) | Tab focuses; Enter activates; Space activates | none required (native role) | 2.1.1, 4.1.2 | | PrimaryButton (`disabled`) | Button | Skipped in Tab order (native `disabled` removes from tab sequence) | native `disabled` attr | 4.1.2 | | PrimaryButton (`isLoading`) | Button | Tab focuses; activation should be suppressed while busy | `aria-busy="true"` | 4.1.2, 4.1.3 | **Screen reader experience to verify:** - Default: announces "**[label]**, button" on focus. - Disabled: announces "**[label]**, button, **unavailable**" (NVDA/JAWS) or "**[label]**, dimmed, button" (VoiceOver). The disabled state MUST be conveyed by AT, not by visual dimming alone. - Loading: announces "**[label]**, button, **busy**" — and the spinner must NOT inject decorative text into the announcement. **Decision to confirm during testing — `disabled` vs `aria-disabled`:** The component uses the native HTML `disabled` attribute. Native `disabled` removes the button from the tab order, so a screen-reader user navigating by Tab will never land on it (though they can still reach it in NVDA/JAWS browse mode and hear the state). This is acceptable for this component's contexts. If product requirements ever need the disabled button to remain focusable and discoverable (common for toolbar buttons), that would require `aria-disabled="true"` + suppressed activation instead — flag this to the team but test against the current native-`disabled` implementation. --- ## Focus Management Plan A button does not open overlays, trap focus, or restore focus — so focus management here is limited to **reachability and indicator visibility**. There is no roving tabindex, no focus trap, and no restoration logic to test. 1. **Reachability:** Tab reaches the enabled button; Shift+Tab reaches it in reverse. (Test by key press, not by reading `tabindex`.) 2. **Disabled exclusion:** A `disabled` button is skipped by Tab — verify focus moves to the next focusable element. 3. **Loading focusability:** An `isLoading` button still receives focus (it is not `disabled`), so a keyboard user is not stranded mid-flow. Verify it remains in the tab order. 4. **Focus indicator visibility:** The custom 2px-offset brand focus ring must be visible on keyboard focus and meet **3:1 contrast against the adjacent background** per WCAG 2.4.11 Focus Appearance / 1.4.11 Non-text Contrast. Verify the ring appears on keyboard focus and is not suppressed by `:focus { outline: none }` without a `:focus-visible` replacement. — WCAG 2.4.7 Focus Visible, 2.4.11 Focus Appearance. --- ## State Communication Design ### State Communication Table | State | Visual | Programmatic | ARIA / attr | WCAG | |-------|--------|-------------|-------------|------| | Default / idle | Brand fill, label, optional icon | native button role | — | 4.1.2 | | Focused (keyboard) | 2px brand focus ring, 3:1 contrast | browser focus | — | 2.4.7, 2.4.11 | | Disabled | Dimmed fill | removed from tab order; state exposed to AT | native `disabled` | 1.4.1, 4.1.2 | | Loading / busy | Spinner replaces icon | busy state exposed to AT | `aria-busy="true"` | 4.1.2, 4.1.3 | **Key state-communication assertions:** - **No color-only signaling.** Disabled state must be conveyed beyond the dimmed color — the native `disabled` attribute carries it to AT, satisfying WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color. Verify AT announces it; do not rely on the dim alone. - **Spinner is not announced as text.** The spinner element must be `aria-hidden="true"` so `aria-busy` is the only loading signal AT receives. A spinner that renders an accessible name (e.g., an SVG with a ``, or text like "Loading...") will produce double or noisy announcements — test for this explicitly. — WCAG 1.1.1, 4.1.2. - **`aria-busy` placement.** Confirm `aria-busy="true"` lands on the `<button>` itself in the rendered DOM, not on a wrapper. (DOM verification step below.) There is no live region requirement for this component in isolation: `aria-busy` on the button communicates the per-control loading state to AT on focus. If the host form needs to announce "submitting…" globally, that live region belongs to the form, not the button — out of scope here. --- ## Visual Accessibility Plan 1. **Label/text contrast (WCAG 1.4.3):** Button label text vs. button fill ≥ 4.5:1 (normal text) or ≥ 3:1 (large text, 18px+ or 14px+ bold). Test default and disabled fills — the dimmed disabled fill is the at-risk case. 2. **Focus ring contrast (WCAG 1.4.11 / 2.4.11):** 2px ring vs. adjacent background ≥ 3:1. The "adjacent background" is whatever sits behind the 2px offset — test in each host context (form, inline, toolbar) since backgrounds differ. 3. **Non-text component contrast (WCAG 1.4.11):** The button's boundary against the page background ≥ 3:1 so the control is perceivable. 4. **Touch target (WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size, AA):** Button ≥ 24×24 CSS px (AA minimum). Note the stricter 44×44 is a AAA target — measure and report against the 24px AA bar, flag if under 44 for touch-heavy contexts. 5. **Disabled-state contrast caveat:** WCAG exempts *inactive* (disabled) controls from the contrast minimum (1.4.3, 1.4.11). Do not fail the build on dimmed-disabled contrast — but **do** verify the disabled state is still programmatically exposed (above), since exemption from contrast is not exemption from AT communication. --- ## Content Accessibility Plan 1. **Accessible name quality (WCAG 2.4.4 / 4.1.2):** Label is descriptive of the action ("Save changes," not "OK" where ambiguous). When `aria-label` overrides the visible text, the override must still describe the action and should contain the visible label text where one exists — WCAG 2.5.3 Label in Name. 2. **Icon handling (WCAG 1.1.1):** Leading icon is decorative → `aria-hidden="true"`, contributing nothing to the name. If a button is icon-only (no visible text), the accessible name MUST come from `aria-label` on the button — verify no icon-only instance ships with an empty accessible name. 3. **Spinner content (WCAG 1.1.1):** Spinner is decorative → `aria-hidden="true"`; the loading semantics come from `aria-busy`, not from spinner text. --- ## Testing Strategy ### 1. Automated testing (Playwright + axe-core, runs in CI on every PR) Render the component in **each of four state variants** (default, focused, disabled, loading) — axe scans a single rendered state, so each state needs its own scan. Inject axe-core (`@axe-core/playwright`) and assert zero violations for these specific rules: | Rule ID | Why it applies | States to scan | |---------|----------------|----------------| | `button-name` | Button must have an accessible name (text or `aria-label`) | all four | | `color-contrast` | Label/fill ≥ 4.5:1 (or 3:1 large) | default, loading, focused (skip disabled — exempt) | | `aria-allowed-attr` | `aria-busy`, `aria-label` must be valid on `<button>` | loading, default | | `aria-hidden-focus` | Hidden icon/spinner must not be focusable / must not hide focusable content | default, loading | **What axe-core will NOT catch** (and therefore needs manual/Playwright-scripted coverage): focus-ring visibility and its 3:1 contrast, keyboard activation actually firing the handler, the *quality* of the accessible name, and whether the screen reader speaks "unavailable"/"busy." Automated absence-of-violations is necessary, not sufficient. **Playwright ARIA-attribute assertions (syntax to use):** ```js // Loading state exposes aria-busy on the button itself await expect(page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Save changes' })) .toHaveAttribute('aria-busy', 'true'); // Disabled state — assert the native property, NOT a tabindex value await expect(page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Save changes' })) .toBeDisabled(); // Accessible name resolves from aria-label override when set await expect(page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Close dialog' })).toBeVisible(); // Icon is excluded from the name (button announces label only) await expect(page.getByRole('button')).toHaveAccessibleName('Save changes'); ``` ### 2. Keyboard interaction testing (Playwright, real key events) Step-by-step script — run for the **default** and **loading** states (disabled is verified by exclusion): 1. Load the component story/page. Press **Tab** until focus reaches the button. 2. **Verify the focus ring is visible** (screenshot the focused state; compare against the focus-ring baseline — see visual regression below). 3. Press **Enter** → assert the click handler fired (e.g., a spy, a navigation, or a visible result). 4. Re-focus, press **Space** → assert the handler fired again. (Native buttons activate on **both** Enter and Space — test both, per APG Button.) 5. **Disabled state:** Tab through the surrounding UI → assert focus **skips** the button (the next focusable element receives focus instead). 6. **Loading state:** Tab to the button → assert it receives focus; press Enter/Space → assert activation is suppressed while `aria-busy="true"` (no duplicate submit). Use real `page.keyboard.press('Tab' | 'Enter' | 'Space')` — never synthetic `click()` for keyboard-path verification. ### 3. Screen reader testing (manual — keystroke-explicit for a team new to SR) Run the matrix below once per release / at the a11y-critic checkpoint, not per-commit. Test each state in each SR. **NVDA (Windows + Firefox):** 1. Start NVDA (Ctrl+Alt+N). Load the page. 2. Press **Insert+F7** to open the **Elements List**; choose **Buttons** to confirm the button appears with its expected name. 3. Tab to the button. **Listen for:** "**[label]**, button." 4. Disabled variant — navigate to it in browse mode (↓ arrow). **Listen for:** "**[label]**, button, **unavailable**." 5. Loading variant — Tab to it. **Listen for:** "**[label]**, button, **busy**." Confirm **no** spurious "loading"/"image"/spinner text. **JAWS (Windows + Chrome):** 1. Start JAWS. Load the page. Press **Insert+F5** to list form fields / **Insert+Ctrl+B** to cycle buttons, confirming the name. 2. Tab to the button. **Listen for:** "**[label]**, button." 3. Disabled: **Listen for:** "**[label]**, button, **unavailable**." 4. Loading: **Listen for:** "**[label]**, button, **busy**." **VoiceOver (macOS + Safari):** 1. Start VO (Cmd+F5). Use **VO+U** (rotor) → **Form Controls** to confirm the button is listed with its name. 2. Navigate to the button with **VO+→**. **Listen for:** "**[label]**, button." 3. Activate with **VO+Space** → assert the action fires. 4. Disabled: **Listen for:** "**[label]**, **dimmed**, button." 5. Loading: confirm the busy state is conveyed and the spinner is silent. ### 4. Visual regression testing (Playwright screenshots — component-scoped, not full page) Capture **component-only** snapshots (clip to the button's bounding box, not the page) for: default, hover, **keyboard-focused** (focus ring must be present in the baseline), disabled, and loading. The focused snapshot is the load-bearing one — it is the only automated guard against a future `outline: none` regression. Do not snapshot the whole page; full-page baselines are brittle and hide the component-level change that matters. ### 5. Acceptance criteria (measurable) A PR passes accessibility review when ALL hold: - [ ] axe-core reports **zero** violations for `button-name`, `color-contrast`, `aria-allowed-attr`, `aria-hidden-focus` across default, disabled, and loading renders. - [ ] Tab reaches the enabled button; **Enter and Space both** fire the handler (verified by real key events, not `click()`). - [ ] Focus ring is **visible** on keyboard focus and measures **≥ 3:1 contrast** against the adjacent background in every host context. - [ ] `disabled` button is **skipped by Tab**, and AT announces it as **"unavailable"** (NVDA/JAWS) or **"dimmed"** (VoiceOver). - [ ] `isLoading` button renders **`aria-busy="true"` on the `<button>` element** (confirmed in rendered DOM), AT announces **"busy,"** and the spinner produces **no** decorative text. - [ ] Label text contrast ≥ **4.5:1** (or 3:1 large) in default and loading states. - [ ] Accessible name resolves correctly from visible text, and from `aria-label` when the override is set, with no icon/spinner noise (`toHaveAccessibleName` assertion passes). - [ ] Component-scoped visual baselines exist for default, focused, disabled, and loading; the **focused** baseline shows the ring. **DOM verification step (required — not satisfied by axe + screenshots alone):** In the loading and `aria-label`-override cases, inspect the **rendered** DOM (Playwright `page.locator(...).evaluate(el => el.outerHTML)` or DevTools) and confirm: `aria-busy="true"` is on the `<button>` (not a wrapper), `aria-label` is on the `<button>`, and the icon/spinner carries `aria-hidden="true"`. This catches the class of bug where an attribute is authored on the wrong element and every automated check still passes. --- ## Implementation Tasks ### Task 1: Automated axe-core + ARIA-attribute suite 🔍 **Review checkpoint** **Files:** `PrimaryButton.a11y.spec.ts` (Playwright) **Structure stub:** Render `<PrimaryButton>` in 4 states (default, disabled, loading, focused); inject `@axe-core/playwright`; assert per-rule. **ARIA attributes to assert:** `aria-busy="true"` (loading) — WCAG 4.1.2/4.1.3; native `disabled` via `toBeDisabled()` — WCAG 4.1.2; accessible name via `toHaveAccessibleName` — WCAG 4.1.2. **Keyboard interactions:** n/a (covered in Task 2). **Tests:** zero violations for `button-name`, `color-contrast`, `aria-allowed-attr`, `aria-hidden-focus`; `aria-busy` present in loading; `toBeDisabled()` in disabled. **WCAG criteria:** 1.4.3, 4.1.2, 1.1.1. ### Task 2: Keyboard activation + focus suite 🔍 **Review checkpoint** **Files:** `PrimaryButton.keyboard.spec.ts` **Structure stub:** Tab to button → screenshot focus → Enter (assert handler) → Space (assert handler) → disabled skip → loading activation-suppressed. **ARIA attributes:** none new; verifies platform behavior. **Keyboard interactions:** Tab, Shift+Tab, Enter, Space — real `page.keyboard.press`. **Tests:** Enter fires; Space fires; disabled skipped; loading focusable but activation suppressed; focus ring present in screenshot. **WCAG criteria:** 2.1.1, 2.4.7, 2.4.11. ### Task 3: Visual regression baselines (component-scoped) 🔍 **Review checkpoint** **Files:** `PrimaryButton.visual.spec.ts` + baseline snapshots **Structure stub:** Clip-to-component screenshots for default, hover, keyboard-focused, disabled, loading. **Tests:** each state matches baseline; focused baseline shows the 2px brand ring. **WCAG criteria:** 2.4.7, 2.4.11, 1.4.11. ### Task 4: Manual screen reader pass (NVDA, JAWS, VoiceOver) 🔍 **Review checkpoint** **Files:** checklist artifact (e.g., `PrimaryButton.sr-checklist.md`) recording pass/fail per SR × state. **Tests:** name/role/state announced per the SR scripts above; disabled → "unavailable"/"dimmed"; loading → "busy" with silent spinner. **WCAG criteria:** 4.1.2, 4.1.3, 1.1.1. --- ## a11y-Critic Review Checkpoints | Checkpoint | After Task | Focus | |-----------|-----------|-------| | 🔍 1 | Task 1 | Right axe rules chosen for a button; each state scanned (not just default); ARIA assertions target the `<button>` element; no bogus `tabindex` assertion | | 🔍 2 | Task 2 | Both Enter and Space tested; real key events (not `click()`); disabled exclusion and loading activation-suppression covered | | 🔍 3 | Task 3 | Snapshots are component-scoped, not full-page; focused baseline actually captures the ring | | 🔍 4 | Task 4 | SR scripts are keystroke-explicit and executable by a non-SR-expert; disabled and loading states verified in all three SRs, not just default | **When a11y-critic is triggered in the workflow:** (1) when this test plan is first implemented (review the suite before it's trusted in CI), and (2) whenever the `PrimaryButton` component itself changes its rendering, ARIA, focus styles, or state handling — a component diff re-opens the design questions the tests encode. Routine green CI runs do not require the critic; a change to the button's accessibility surface does. --- ### Contract Appendix ### Architecture Overview Four-layer test strategy for a native `<button>`: (1) automated axe-core + Playwright ARIA assertions on all four state renders, gating every PR in CI; (2) real-key-event keyboard suite covering Enter+Space activation, disabled tab-exclusion, and loading activation-suppression; (3) component-scoped visual regression with a focus-ring baseline as the guard against `outline:none` regressions; (4) a keystroke-explicit manual SR pass (NVDA/JAWS/VoiceOver) at the review checkpoint. The design choice throughout is to lean on native-element semantics (role, focusability, Enter/Space) and verify they are preserved — never to re-implement or to assert `tabindex`. ### Implementation Tasks #### Task 1: Automated axe-core + ARIA-attribute suite Estimated Effort: low Depends on: none #### Test Strategy for Task 1 axe-core scans of all four states for `button-name`, `color-contrast`, `aria-allowed-attr`, `aria-hidden-focus`; Playwright assertions for `aria-busy`, `toBeDisabled()`, `toHaveAccessibleName`. WCAG 1.4.3, 4.1.2, 1.1.1. #### Acceptance Criteria for Task 1 Zero violations for the four named rules across default/disabled/loading; `aria-busy="true"` confirmed on the `<button>` in loading; accessible name resolves with no icon/spinner noise. #### Task 2: Keyboard activation + focus suite Estimated Effort: low Depends on: none #### Test Strategy for Task 2 Real `page.keyboard.press` for Tab/Shift+Tab/Enter/Space; assert handler fires on both Enter and Space; disabled skipped; loading focusable but activation suppressed; focus screenshot. WCAG 2.1.1, 2.4.7, 2.4.11. #### Acceptance Criteria for Task 2 Enter and Space both fire the handler; disabled button skipped by Tab; loading button focusable with activation suppressed; focus ring visible on keyboard focus. #### Task 3: Visual regression baselines Estimated Effort: low Depends on: Task 2 (reuses focused-state navigation) #### Test Strategy for Task 3 Component-clipped screenshots for default/hover/focused/disabled/loading; focused baseline must contain the ring. WCAG 2.4.7, 2.4.11, 1.4.11. #### Acceptance Criteria for Task 3 Each state matches its component-scoped baseline; focused baseline shows the 2px brand ring; no full-page snapshots. #### Task 4: Manual screen reader pass Estimated Effort: medium Depends on: Task 1 (DOM/ARIA confirmed correct before SR pass) #### Test Strategy for Task 4 Execute the NVDA (Insert+F7), JAWS (Insert+Ctrl+B), and VoiceOver (VO+U rotor, VO+Space activate) scripts against default/disabled/loading; record per-cell pass/fail. WCAG 4.1.2, 4.1.3, 1.1.1. #### Acceptance Criteria for Task 4 Name+role announced in all three SRs; disabled announced "unavailable"/"dimmed"; loading announced "busy" with the spinner silent. ### Failure Modes - **Default-state-only testing:** axe and screenshots run on the idle button only; disabled and loading defects ship. → Mitigated by requiring all four state renders in every layer. - **Attribute on the wrong element:** `aria-busy`/`aria-label` authored on a wrapper, not the `<button>`; all automated checks still pass. → Mitigated by the explicit DOM verification step. - **Focus ring silently removed:** a later CSS change adds `outline:none` with no `:focus-visible` replacement. → Mitigated by the focused visual baseline. - **Spinner read as text:** decorative spinner exposes an accessible name, producing noisy/double announcements. → Mitigated by the SR pass listening for spurious spinner text and by `aria-hidden` verification. - **Wrong-platform assertion:** suite asserts `tabindex="0"` on the native button, encoding a false expectation. → Explicitly prohibited; focusability verified by Tab reachability instead.

_benchmark.method: plan 006 Phase D Claude lane: Agent(subagent_type=a11y-planner, model=opus), one background subagent per fixture; verbatim PLANNER_PROMPT_PREFIX task; agent wrote plan via Write tool; orchestrator wrapped to response JSON
